tools.title: Update IPv6 Firewall NAT Rule
tools.description: Updates an existing IPv6 firewall NAT rule. Pass "" to clear an optional field (e.g. to_address="").

tools.name: remove_ipv6_nat_rule
tools.title: Remove IPv6 Firewall NAT Rule
tools.description: Removes an IPv6 firewall NAT rule from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: move_ipv6_nat_rule
tools.title: Move IPv6 NAT Rule
tools.description: Moves an IPv6 firewall NAT rule to a different position in the chain.

tools.name: enable_ipv6_nat_rule
tools.title: Enable IPv6 NAT Rule
tools.description: Enables an IPv6 firewall NAT rule.

tools.name: disable_ipv6_nat_rule
tools.title: Disable IPv6 NAT Rule
tools.description: Disables an IPv6 firewall NAT rule.

tools.name: create_ipv6_mangle_rule
tools.title: Create IPv6 Firewall Mangle Rule
tools.description: Creates an IPv6 firewall mangle rule on the MikroTik device. chain: prerouting/input/forward/output/postrouting. action: mark-connection/mark-packet/mark-routing/change-dscp/change-hop-limit/change-mss/accept/etc. Set the matching new-*-mark field for mark-* actions and keep passthrough=true to let later rules also match.

place_before.description: Rule number or ID (*N) to insert before

tools.name: list_ipv6_mangle_rules
tools.title: List IPv6 Firewall Mangle Rules
tools.description: Lists IPv6 firewall mangle rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ipv6_mangle_rule
tools.title: Get IPv6 Firewall Mangle Rule
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific IPv6 firewall mangle rule.

rule_id.description: Rule ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0"

tools.name: update_ipv6_mangle_rule
tools.title: Update IPv6 Firewall Mangle Rule
tools.description: Updates an existing IPv6 firewall mangle rule. Pass "" to clear an optional field.

tools.name: remove_ipv6_mangle_rule
tools.title: Remove IPv6 Firewall Mangle Rule
tools.description: Removes an IPv6 firewall mangle rule from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: move_ipv6_mangle_rule
tools.title: Move IPv6 Mangle Rule
tools.description: Moves an IPv6 firewall mangle rule to a different position in the chain.

tools.name: enable_ipv6_mangle_rule
tools.title: Enable IPv6 Mangle Rule
tools.description: Enables an IPv6 firewall mangle rule.

tools.name: disable_ipv6_mangle_rule
tools.title: Disable IPv6 Mangle Rule
tools.description: Disables an IPv6 firewall mangle rule.

tools.name: create_ipv6_raw_rule
tools.title: Create IPv6 Firewall Raw Rule
tools.description: Creates an IPv6 firewall raw rule on the MikroTik device. The raw table runs before connection tracking — use it to bypass tracking (notrack) or drop traffic cheaply. chain: 'prerouting' or 'output'.

place_before.description: Rule number or ID (*N) to insert before

tools.name: list_ipv6_raw_rules
tools.title: List IPv6 Firewall Raw Rules
tools.description: Lists IPv6 firewall raw rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ipv6_raw_rule
tools.title: Get IPv6 Firewall Raw Rule
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific IPv6 firewall raw rule.

rule_id.description: Rule ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0"

tools.name: update_ipv6_raw_rule
tools.title: Update IPv6 Firewall Raw Rule
tools.description: Updates an existing IPv6 firewall raw rule. Pass "" to clear an optional field.

tools.name: remove_ipv6_raw_rule
tools.title: Remove IPv6 Firewall Raw Rule
tools.description: Removes an IPv6 firewall raw rule from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: move_ipv6_raw_rule
tools.title: Move IPv6 Raw Rule
tools.description: Moves an IPv6 firewall raw rule to a different position in the chain.

tools.name: enable_ipv6_raw_rule
tools.title: Enable IPv6 Raw Rule
tools.description: Enables an IPv6 firewall raw rule.

tools.name: disable_ipv6_raw_rule
tools.title: Disable IPv6 Raw Rule
tools.description: Disables an IPv6 firewall raw rule.

tools.name: add_ipv6_address_list_entry
tools.title: Add IPv6 Address-List Entry
tools.description: Adds an IPv6 address/prefix to an IPv6 firewall address-list.

address.description: IPv6 address or prefix to add, e.g. '2001:db8::1' or '2001:db8::/32'

tools.name: list_ipv6_address_lists
tools.title: List IPv6 Address-Lists
tools.description: Lists IPv6 firewall address-list entries with optional filters.

tools.name: get_ipv6_address_list_entry
tools.title: Get IPv6 Address-List Entry
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific IPv6 address-list entry by its internal id.

tools.name: remove_ipv6_address_list_entry
tools.title: Remove IPv6 Address-List Entry
tools.description: Removes an IPv6 address-list entry by its internal id.

tools.name: enable_ipv6_address_list_entry
tools.title: Enable IPv6 Address-List Entry
tools.description: Enables an IPv6 address-list entry by its internal id.

tools.name: disable_ipv6_address_list_entry
tools.title: Disable IPv6 Address-List Entry
tools.description: Disables an IPv6 address-list entry by its internal id.

tools.name: add_ipv6_nd
tools.title: Add IPv6 ND Interface Config
tools.description:
Adds a per-interface IPv6 Neighbor Discovery / Router Advertisement configuration on the MikroTik device.

Notes:
    managed_address_configuration (M flag): tell hosts to use DHCPv6 for
        addresses.
    other_configuration (O flag): tell hosts to use DHCPv6 for other info
        (e.g. DNS).
    ra_interval: min-max range, e.g. '3m20s-10m'.

tools.name: list_ipv6_nd
tools.title: List IPv6 ND Configs
tools.description: Lists IPv6 Neighbor Discovery interface configurations on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ipv6_nd
tools.title: Get IPv6 ND Config
tools.description: Gets detailed IPv6 ND configuration for an interface (or '.id').

tools.name: update_ipv6_nd
tools.title: Update IPv6 ND Config
tools.description: Updates an existing IPv6 ND interface configuration (by interface name or '.id'). Also use this to configure the built-in 'all' entry.

tools.name: remove_ipv6_nd
tools.title: Remove IPv6 ND Config
tools.description: Removes a per-interface IPv6 ND configuration (the built-in 'all' entry cannot be removed).

tools.name: add_ipv6_nd_prefix
tools.title: Add IPv6 ND Prefix
tools.description:
Adds an advertised IPv6 ND prefix on the MikroTik device.

Notes:
    autonomous: allow hosts to auto-configure addresses from this prefix
        (SLAAC, the A flag).

tools.name: list_ipv6_nd_prefixes
tools.title: List IPv6 ND Prefixes
tools.description: Lists advertised IPv6 ND prefixes on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: remove_ipv6_nd_prefix
tools.title: Remove IPv6 ND Prefix
tools.description: Removes an advertised IPv6 ND prefix by ID or prefix value from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_ipv6_neighbors
tools.title: List IPv6 Neighbors
tools.description: Lists IPv6 neighbor cache entries (discovered via Neighbor Discovery) on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ipv6_neighbor
tools.title: Get IPv6 Neighbor
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific IPv6 neighbor by ID or address.

tools.name: remove_ipv6_neighbor
tools.title: Remove IPv6 Neighbor
tools.description: Flushes an IPv6 neighbor cache entry by ID or address. The entry may be re-learned automatically via Neighbor Discovery.

tools.name: create_ipv6_pool
tools.title: Create IPv6 Pool
tools.description:
Creates an IPv6 pool on the MikroTik device.

Notes:
    prefix: the overall block, e.g. '2001:db8::/48'.
    prefix_length: size of each delegation carved from the block, e.g.
        64. Must be longer than the pool prefix.

prefix_length.description: Size of each carved delegation, e.g. 64

tools.name: list_ipv6_pools
tools.title: List IPv6 Pools
tools.description: Lists IPv6 pools on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ipv6_pool
tools.title: Get IPv6 Pool
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific IPv6 pool.

tools.name: list_ipv6_pool_used
tools.title: List IPv6 Pool Usage
tools.description: Lists the currently delegated prefixes taken from IPv6 pools (`/ipv6 pool used`).

tools.name: update_ipv6_pool
tools.title: Update IPv6 Pool
tools.description: Updates an existing IPv6 pool on the MikroTik device. Pass comment="" to clear the comment.

tools.name: remove_ipv6_pool
tools.title: Remove IPv6 Pool
tools.description: Removes an IPv6 pool from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: add_ipv6_route
tools.title: Add IPv6 Route
tools.description:
Adds a static IPv6 route on the MikroTik device.

Notes:
    dst_address: destination prefix, e.g. '2001:db8:1::/64'.
    type: 'unicast' (default) routes via gateway; 'blackhole',
        'unreachable' and 'prohibit' discard traffic (no gateway needed).
    routing_table: target a named routing table/FIB.

gateway.description: Next-hop IPv6 address or interface (required for unicast)

tools.name: add_ipv6_default_route
tools.title: Add IPv6 Default Route
tools.description: Adds a default IPv6 route (::/0) via the given gateway on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_ipv6_routes
tools.title: List IPv6 Routes
tools.description: Lists IPv6 routes on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_ipv6_route
tools.title: Get IPv6 Route
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific IPv6 route by ID or destination.

tools.name: remove_ipv6_route
tools.title: Remove IPv6 Route
tools.description: Removes a static IPv6 route by ID from the MikroTik device. Use the .id from list output (e.g. '*5') to avoid ambiguity when multiple routes share a destination.

tools.name: enable_ipv6_route
tools.title: Enable IPv6 Route
tools.description: Enables an IPv6 route by .id.

tools.name: disable_ipv6_route
tools.title: Disable IPv6 Route
tools.description: Disables an IPv6 route by .id.

tools.name: get_ipv6_settings
tools.title: Get IPv6 Settings
tools.description: Gets the global IPv6 settings of the MikroTik device (`/ipv6 settings`).

tools.name: update_ipv6_settings
tools.title: Update IPv6 Settings
tools.description:
Updates the global IPv6 settings of the MikroTik device.

Notes:
    disable_ipv6: when true, IPv6 is turned off device-wide.
    forward: enable/disable IPv6 packet forwarding (routing).
    accept_redirects / accept_router_advertisements: 'yes', 'no', or
        'yes-if-forwarding-disabled' (the host-like default).

tools.name: list_routing_ids
tools.title: List Routing IDs
tools.description: Lists Router-ID instances (`/routing id`). Each instance assigns a stable 32-bit router identifier to a routing process (OSPF/BGP) and can auto-select the ID from a chosen interface or loopback.

name_filter.description: Substring match on the instance name

tools.name: get_routing_id
tools.title: Get Routing ID
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific Router-ID instance by name.

tools.name: add_routing_id
tools.title: Add Routing ID
tools.description: Adds a Router-ID instance. Either pin a fixed `id` (an IPv4 address) or let RouterOS pick one dynamically from an interface/loopback via `select_dynamic_id`.

name.description: Unique instance name, referenced by OSPF/BGP

id.description: Fixed router-id as an IPv4 address, e.g. '10.0.0.1'

select_dynamic_id.description: Interface/loopback name to auto-derive the id from when `id` is omitted

tools.name: update_routing_id
tools.title: Update Routing ID
tools.description: Updates a Router-ID instance. Pass "" to `id` or `select_dynamic_id` to clear that property.

tools.name: remove_routing_id
tools.title: Remove Routing ID
tools.description: Removes a Router-ID instance by name.

tools.name: set_routing_id_enabled
tools.title: Enable/Disable Routing ID
tools.description: Enables or disables a Router-ID instance by name.

enabled.description: true to enable, false to disable

tools.name: get_routing_settings
tools.title: Get Routing Settings
tools.description: Shows global routing settings (`/routing settings`): ECMP/multipath hash policy for IPv4 and IPv6 and whether VRFs are treated as interfaces.

tools.name: update_routing_settings
tools.title: Update Routing Settings
tools.description: Updates global routing settings. `*_multipath_hash_policy` controls how ECMP next-hops are chosen (l3 = src/dst IP, l3-inner = inner header for tunnels, l4 = include L4 ports). `ipv4_vrf_as_interface` exposes VRFs as interfaces to the rest of the config.

tools.name: list_routing_tables
tools.title: List Routing Tables
tools.description: Lists routing tables (`/routing table`). Each named table is a separate RIB; set `fib` on a table to also install its routes into the forwarding plane (FIB). The built-in `main` table is always present.

tools.name: get_routing_table_def
tools.title: Get Routing Table
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific routing table definition by name.

tools.name: add_routing_table
tools.title: Add Routing Table
tools.description: Adds a named routing table. Enable `fib` to install the table's routes into the forwarding plane (otherwise the table is RIB-only and used purely for lookups by routing rules/marks).

name.description: Unique table name, referenced by routes and routing rules

fib.description: Install routes into the FIB (forwarding plane)

tools.name: update_routing_table
tools.title: Update Routing Table
tools.description: Updates a routing table's fib flag, comment, or disabled state.

tools.name: remove_routing_table
tools.title: Remove Routing Table
tools.description: Removes a routing table by name. The built-in `main` table cannot be removed.

tools.name: set_routing_table_enabled
tools.title: Enable/Disable Routing Table
tools.description: Enables or disables a routing table by name.

enabled.description: true to enable, false to disable

tools.name: list_routing_rules
tools.title: List Routing Rules
tools.description: Lists policy routing rules (`/routing rule`). Rules are evaluated top-down and pick which routing table a packet is looked up in based on source/destination, interface or routing-mark.

tools.name: get_routing_rule
tools.title: Get Routing Rule
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific routing rule by its internal id.

rule_id.description: Rule id from list output, e.g. "*3"

tools.name: add_routing_rule
tools.title: Add Routing Rule
tools.description: Adds a policy routing rule. Match on source/destination prefix, incoming interface and/or routing-mark; `action=lookup` resolves in `table` (and continues to lower-priority tables if no match), `lookup-only-in-table` stops at that table, `drop`/`unreachable` discard the packet.

table.description: Target routing table for lookup actions

place_before.description: Insert before this rule id to control ordering

tools.name: update_routing_rule
tools.title: Update Routing Rule
tools.description: Updates a routing rule by id. Pass "" to src_address, dst_address, routing_mark, interface or table to clear.

tools.name: remove_routing_rule
tools.title: Remove Routing Rule
tools.description: Removes a routing rule by id.

tools.name: set_routing_rule_enabled
tools.title: Enable/Disable Routing Rule
tools.description: Enables or disables a routing rule by id.

enabled.description: true to enable, false to disable

tools.name: list_routing_nexthops
tools.title: List Routing Next-hops
tools.description: Lists resolved routing next-hops (`/routing nexthop`). This is the recursive next-hop resolution table: it shows how each gateway resolves to a concrete interface + immediate gateway, which routes reference it, and whether it is currently active. Read-only and diagnostic — useful for debugging recursive/BGP next-hops.

gateway_filter.description: Substring match on the resolved gateway address

tools.name: get_routing_nexthop_stats
tools.title: Routing Next-hop Statistics
tools.description: Summarises the routing next-hop table: total vs active next-hop count.

tools.name: list_routing_filter_rules
tools.title: List Routing Filter Rules
tools.description: Lists routing filter rules (`/routing filter rule`). Rules belong to a named chain and are written as a script-like expression, e.g. `if (dst in 10.0.0.0/8) { set distance 30; accept }`. Chains are referenced by BGP/OSPF as input/output filters and by `select-rule` matchers.

chain_filter.description: Show only rules in this chain

tools.name: add_routing_filter_rule
tools.title: Add Routing Filter Rule
tools.description: Adds a routing filter rule to a chain. `rule` is the full match/action expression; create the chain implicitly by naming it here, then reference it from a BGP/OSPF input/output filter.

rule.description: Match/action expression, e.g. "if (dst-len <= 24) { accept } else { reject }"

place_before.description: Insert before this rule id to control ordering

tools.name: update_routing_filter_rule
tools.title: Update Routing Filter Rule
tools.description: Updates a routing filter rule's chain, expression, comment, or disabled state by id.

tools.name: remove_routing_filter_rule
tools.title: Remove Routing Filter Rule
tools.description: Removes a routing filter rule by id.

tools.name: set_routing_filter_rule_enabled
tools.title: Enable/Disable Routing Filter Rule
tools.description: Enables or disables a routing filter rule by id.

tools.name: list_routing_filter_select_rules
tools.title: List Routing Filter Select-rules
tools.description: Lists routing filter select-rules (`/routing filter select-rule`). Select-rules choose which filter `chain` to jump into based on prefix/length conditions — the structured front-end to the script chains.

tools.name: list_routing_filter_num_lists
tools.title: List Routing Filter Num-lists
tools.description: Lists routing filter num-lists (`/routing filter num-list`). A num-list is a named set of numeric ranges (AS numbers, communities, prefix lengths) that filter rules can match against by name.

list_filter.description: Show only entries of this named list

tools.name: add_routing_filter_num_list
tools.title: Add Routing Filter Num-list Entry
tools.description: Adds a numeric range entry to a named num-list.

range.description: Numeric range or single value, e.g. "65000-65010" or "100"

tools.name: remove_routing_filter_num_list
tools.title: Remove Routing Filter Num-list Entry
tools.description: Removes a num-list entry by id.

tools.name: list_bfd_configurations
tools.title: List BFD Configurations
tools.description: Lists BFD configuration entries (`/routing bfd configuration`). BFD gives sub-second failure detection for a link/neighbor so routing protocols (OSPF/BGP) can tear down a session far faster than their own hold timers. Each entry binds timers to a set of interfaces in a VRF.

tools.name: add_bfd_configuration
tools.title: Add BFD Configuration
tools.description: Adds a BFD configuration. `interfaces` selects where BFD runs (an interface or interface-list); `min_rx`/`min_tx` are the desired minimum receive/transmit intervals and `multiplier` is how many missed packets declare the session down (detection time ≈ interval × multiplier).

interfaces.description: Interface or interface-list name BFD runs on

tools.name: update_bfd_configuration
tools.title: Update BFD Configuration
tools.description: Updates a BFD configuration entry by id.

tools.name: remove_bfd_configuration
tools.title: Remove BFD Configuration
tools.description: Removes a BFD configuration entry by id.

tools.name: set_bfd_configuration_enabled
tools.title: Enable/Disable BFD Configuration
tools.description: Enables or disables a BFD configuration entry by id.

tools.name: list_bfd_sessions
tools.title: List BFD Sessions
tools.description: Lists live BFD sessions (`/routing bfd session`): each neighbor's state (up/down), local/remote discriminators and negotiated timers. Read-only — use it to confirm BFD is actually up before relying on it for fast failover.

tools.name: list_bgp_connections
tools.title: List BGP Connections
tools.description: Lists BGP connections (`/routing bgp connection`). In RouterOS v7 a 'connection' is one configured peer (or listener) that pulls common settings from optional templates. Shows local/remote AS, addresses and role.

tools.name: get_bgp_connection
tools.title: Get BGP Connection
tools.description: Gets detailed configuration for a specific BGP connection by name.

tools.name: add_bgp_connection
tools.title: Add BGP Connection
tools.description: Adds a BGP connection (peer). At minimum give `name`, the peer `remote_address`/`remote_as`, the local `as` and a `local_role` (ebgp or ibgp). Input/output route filters reference `/routing filter` chains. Common settings can be factored into a template referenced via `templates`.

local_role.description: local.role: ebgp, ibgp, ebgp-customer, ebgp-provider, ibgp-rr, ibgp-rr-client, …

templates.description: Template name(s) to inherit settings from

input_filter.description: Routing filter chain for inbound routes (input.filter)

output_filter.description: Routing filter chain for outbound routes (output.filter)

routing_table.description: RIB to install learned routes into

tools.name: update_bgp_connection
tools.title: Update BGP Connection
tools.description: Updates settings of an existing BGP connection by name.

tools.name: remove_bgp_connection
tools.title: Remove BGP Connection
tools.description: Removes a BGP connection by name (tears down the peering).

tools.name: set_bgp_connection_enabled
tools.title: Enable/Disable BGP Connection
tools.description: Enables or disables a BGP connection by name.

tools.name: list_bgp_templates
tools.title: List BGP Templates
tools.description: Lists BGP templates (`/routing bgp template`). Templates hold shared settings (AS, address-families, filters, timers) that connections inherit via their `templates` property.

tools.name: add_bgp_template
tools.title: Add BGP Template
tools.description: Adds a BGP template carrying shared peer settings.

tools.name: remove_bgp_template
tools.title: Remove BGP Template
tools.description: Removes a BGP template by name.

tools.name: list_bgp_sessions
tools.title: List BGP Sessions
tools.description: Lists active BGP sessions (`/routing bgp session`): negotiated state (established/idle/…), remote AS, uptime and prefix counts. Read-only — the authoritative view of which peerings are actually up.

tools.name: list_bgp_advertisements
tools.title: List BGP Advertisements
tools.description: Lists prefixes advertised to BGP peers (`/routing bgp advertisements`). Read-only — useful to confirm exactly what this router is sending to a given peer after output filters are applied.

peer_filter.description: Substring match on the peer name

tools.name: list_ospf_instances
tools.title: List OSPF Instances
tools.description: Lists OSPF instances (`/routing ospf instance`). An instance is one OSPF process: it fixes the protocol version (2 for IPv4, 3 for IPv6), router-id, redistribution and import/export filter chains.

tools.name: add_ospf_instance
tools.title: Add OSPF Instance
tools.description: Adds an OSPF instance. `version` 2 = OSPFv2 (IPv4), 3 = OSPFv3 (IPv6). `router_id` may be an IPv4 address, 'main', or the name of a `/routing id`. `redistribute` is a comma list (connected,static,rip,bgp,…).

router_id.description: IPv4 address, 'main', or a /routing id name

in_filter_chain.description: Routing filter chain for received routes

out_filter_chain.description: Routing filter chain for redistributed routes

tools.name: update_ospf_instance
tools.title: Update OSPF Instance
tools.description: Updates an OSPF instance by name.

tools.name: remove_ospf_instance
tools.title: Remove OSPF Instance
tools.description: Removes an OSPF instance by name.

tools.name: list_ospf_areas
tools.title: List OSPF Areas
tools.description: Lists OSPF areas (`/routing ospf area`). An area groups links inside an instance; `type` controls LSA flooding (default/backbone, stub, nssa).

tools.name: add_ospf_area
tools.title: Add OSPF Area
tools.description: Adds an OSPF area to an instance. The backbone is area-id 0.0.0.0.

area_id.description: Area id in IPv4 notation, e.g. "0.0.0.0" for backbone

instance.description: OSPF instance name this area belongs to

no_summaries.description: Make a totally-stubby/NSSA area (block summary LSAs)

tools.name: remove_ospf_area
tools.title: Remove OSPF Area
tools.description: Removes an OSPF area by name.

tools.name: list_ospf_area_ranges
tools.title: List OSPF Area Ranges
tools.description: Lists OSPF area ranges (`/routing ospf area range`): aggregate prefixes advertised at an area boundary to summarise intra-area routes.

tools.name: add_ospf_area_range
tools.title: Add OSPF Area Range
tools.description: Adds a summarisation range to an OSPF area.

tools.name: remove_ospf_area_range
tools.title: Remove OSPF Area Range
tools.description: Removes an OSPF area range by id.

tools.name: list_ospf_interface_templates
tools.title: List OSPF Interface Templates
tools.description: Lists OSPF interface templates (`/routing ospf interface-template`). A template binds interfaces/networks to an area and sets per-link parameters (cost, type, timers, authentication, passive).

tools.name: add_ospf_interface_template
tools.title: Add OSPF Interface Template
tools.description: Adds an OSPF interface template. Match links via `interfaces` and/or `networks`; `type` sets the link model (broadcast/ptp/nbma/ptmp), `passive` advertises the subnet without forming adjacencies, and the `auth_*` fields enable per-interface authentication.

area.description: OSPF area name to attach matched interfaces to

networks.description: Network prefix(es) to enable OSPF on, e.g. "10.0.0.0/24"

tools.name: remove_ospf_interface_template
tools.title: Remove OSPF Interface Template
tools.description: Removes an OSPF interface template by id.

tools.name: list_ospf_neighbors
tools.title: List OSPF Neighbors
tools.description: Lists OSPF neighbors (`/routing ospf neighbor`): adjacency state (Full/2-Way/…), neighbor router-id and address. Read-only — the key health check for OSPF adjacencies.

tools.name: list_ospf_lsa
tools.title: List OSPF LSAs
tools.description: Lists the OSPF link-state database (`/routing ospf lsa`): every LSA the router holds, by type, area and originator. Read-only — used to inspect topology and diagnose flooding/summarisation problems.

area_filter.description: Show only LSAs for this area

tools.name: list_rip_instances
tools.title: List RIP Instances
tools.description: Lists RIP instances (`/routing rip instance`). An instance is one RIP process with its own router-id, redistribution and import/export filter chains.

tools.name: add_rip_instance
tools.title: Add RIP Instance
tools.description: Adds a RIP instance. `redistribute` is a comma list (connected,static,ospf,bgp,…); filter chains reference `/routing filter`.

router_id.description: IPv4 address, 'main', or a /routing id name

tools.name: update_rip_instance
tools.title: Update RIP Instance
tools.description: Updates a RIP instance by name.

tools.name: remove_rip_instance
tools.title: Remove RIP Instance
tools.description: Removes a RIP instance by name.

tools.name: list_rip_interface_templates
tools.title: List RIP Interface Templates
tools.description: Lists RIP interface templates (`/routing rip interface-template`): which interfaces participate in an instance and their per-link options (passive, authentication, key-chain).

tools.name: add_rip_interface_template
tools.title: Add RIP Interface Template
tools.description: Adds a RIP interface template binding interfaces to an instance. `passive` advertises without sending updates; `key_chain` enables authentication.

tools.name: remove_rip_interface_template
tools.title: Remove RIP Interface Template
tools.description: Removes a RIP interface template by id.

tools.name: list_rip_static_neighbors
tools.title: List RIP Static Neighbors
tools.description: Lists statically-configured RIP neighbors (`/routing rip static-neighbor`) — used to unicast RIP updates to peers across non-broadcast links.

tools.name: add_rip_static_neighbor
tools.title: Add RIP Static Neighbor
tools.description: Adds a static RIP neighbor to unicast updates to.

tools.name: remove_rip_static_neighbor
tools.title: Remove RIP Static Neighbor
tools.description: Removes a RIP static neighbor by id.

tools.name: list_rip_neighbors
tools.title: List RIP Neighbors
tools.description: Lists discovered RIP neighbors (`/routing rip neighbor`): peers this router is exchanging routes with, with last-update timing. Read-only.

tools.name: list_pimsm_instances
tools.title: List PIM-SM Instances
tools.description: Lists PIM Sparse-Mode instances (`/routing pimsm instance`). PIM-SM builds multicast distribution trees rooted at a Rendezvous Point (RP). An instance fixes the address family and VRF the protocol runs in.

tools.name: add_pimsm_instance
tools.title: Add PIM-SM Instance
tools.description: Adds a PIM Sparse-Mode instance.

tools.name: remove_pimsm_instance
tools.title: Remove PIM-SM Instance
tools.description: Removes a PIM-SM instance by name.

tools.name: list_pimsm_interface_templates
tools.title: List PIM-SM Interface Templates
tools.description: Lists PIM-SM interface templates (`/routing pimsm interface-template`): which interfaces run PIM and their hello/priority settings (DR election).

tools.name: add_pimsm_interface_template
tools.title: Add PIM-SM Interface Template
tools.description: Adds a PIM-SM interface template binding interfaces to an instance.

tools.name: remove_pimsm_interface_template
tools.title: Remove PIM-SM Interface Template
tools.description: Removes a PIM-SM interface template by id.

tools.name: list_pimsm_rps
tools.title: List PIM-SM Rendezvous Points
tools.description: Lists PIM-SM Rendezvous Points (`/routing pimsm rp`): the RP addresses and the multicast group ranges each serves as the root of the shared tree.

tools.name: add_pimsm_rp
tools.title: Add PIM-SM Rendezvous Point
tools.description: Adds a static Rendezvous Point for a multicast group range.

tools.name: remove_pimsm_rp
tools.title: Remove PIM-SM Rendezvous Point
tools.description: Removes a PIM-SM Rendezvous Point by id.

tools.name: list_pimsm_neighbors
tools.title: List PIM-SM Neighbors
tools.description: Lists PIM-SM neighbors (`/routing pimsm neighbor`): adjacent PIM routers discovered via Hello messages, with their DR priority and timers. Read-only.

tools.name: get_igmp_proxy_settings
tools.title: Get IGMP Proxy Settings
tools.description: Shows global IGMP-proxy settings (`/routing igmp-proxy`). IGMP proxy forwards multicast between a single upstream and one or more downstream interfaces without a full multicast routing protocol — ideal for IPTV.

tools.name: update_igmp_proxy_settings
tools.title: Update IGMP Proxy Settings
tools.description: Updates global IGMP-proxy settings. `quick_leave` prunes a group immediately on leave (good for IPTV channel zapping); query intervals tune membership refresh behaviour.

tools.name: list_igmp_proxy_interfaces
tools.title: List IGMP Proxy Interfaces
tools.description: Lists IGMP-proxy interfaces (`/routing igmp-proxy interface`). Exactly one interface should be `upstream` (toward the multicast source); the rest are downstream toward receivers.

tools.name: add_igmp_proxy_interface
tools.title: Add IGMP Proxy Interface
tools.description: Adds an interface to the IGMP proxy. Set `upstream=true` for the interface facing the multicast source; `alternative_subnets` whitelists extra source subnets reachable through this interface.

alternative_subnets.description: Comma list of source subnets, e.g. "10.0.0.0/8"

tools.name: update_igmp_proxy_interface
tools.title: Update IGMP Proxy Interface
tools.description: Updates an IGMP-proxy interface by its interface name.

tools.name: remove_igmp_proxy_interface
tools.title: Remove IGMP Proxy Interface
tools.description: Removes an IGMP-proxy interface by its interface name.

tools.name: set_igmp_proxy_interface_enabled
tools.title: Enable/Disable IGMP Proxy Interface
tools.description: Enables or disables an IGMP-proxy interface by name.

tools.name: list_igmp_proxy_mfc
tools.title: List IGMP Proxy Forwarding Cache
tools.description: Lists the IGMP-proxy multicast forwarding cache (`/routing igmp-proxy mfc`): active (source, group) entries and which downstream interfaces each is being forwarded to. Read-only — the live multicast forwarding state.

tools.name: list_gmp_interfaces
tools.title: List GMP Interfaces
tools.description: Lists GMP interfaces (`/routing gmp interface`). GMP is RouterOS's shared Group Management Protocol layer (IGMP for IPv4, MLD for IPv6) used by PIM-SM and IGMP-proxy to learn receiver group memberships. Read-only — shows the querier role, version and timers per interface.

tools.name: list_gmp_group_memberships
tools.title: List GMP Group Memberships
tools.description: Lists GMP group memberships (`/routing gmp group`): the multicast groups currently joined per interface, as learned from IGMP/MLD reports. Read-only — the source of truth for which downstream segments want which groups.

interface_filter.description: Show only memberships on this interface

group_filter.description: Substring match on the multicast group address

tools.name: list_rpki_sessions
tools.title: List RPKI Sessions
tools.description: Lists RPKI sessions (`/routing rpki`). Each session is an RTR connection to a validator cache that streams Validated ROA Payloads (VRPs); BGP filters reference the session `group` to mark routes valid/invalid/unknown for Route Origin Validation. Shows connection status and VRP counts.

group_filter.description: Show only sessions in this group

tools.name: add_rpki_session
tools.title: Add RPKI Session
tools.description: Adds an RPKI session to a validator cache. `group` is the name BGP filters match against; `address`/`port` point at the RTR cache (port 8282 is the common rpki-rtr default). Tune refresh/retry/expire intervals to control how often VRPs are pulled and when stale data is dropped.

group.description: RPKI group name referenced by BGP route filters

address.description: Validator (RTR cache) IP address or hostname

tools.name: update_rpki_session
tools.title: Update RPKI Session
tools.description: Updates an RPKI session by id.

tools.name: remove_rpki_session
tools.title: Remove RPKI Session
tools.description: Removes an RPKI session by id (BGP routes using its group fall back to 'unknown').

tools.name: set_rpki_session_enabled
tools.title: Enable/Disable RPKI Session
tools.description: Enables or disables an RPKI session by id.

tools.name: create_filter_rule
tools.title: Create Firewall Filter Rule
tools.description: Creates a firewall filter rule in the specified chain on the MikroTik device. connection_state: comma-separated e.g. "established,related,new,invalid". limit: RouterOS rate/burst string e.g. "10,5:packet" or "10/1s:packet". tcp_flags: RouterOS flag expression e.g. "syn,!ack". place_before: rule number or ID (*N) to insert before e.g. "0" or "*3".

place_before.description: Rule number or ID (*N) to insert before e.g. "0" or "*3"

tools.name: list_filter_rules
tools.title: List Firewall Filter Rules
tools.description: Lists firewall filter rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_filter_rule
tools.title: Get Firewall Filter Rule
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific firewall filter rule. rule_id: use the ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0".

rule_id.description: Rule ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0"

tools.name: update_filter_rule
tools.title: Update Firewall Filter Rule
tools.description: Updates an existing firewall filter rule on the MikroTik device. rule_id: use the ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0". connection_state: comma-separated e.g. "established,related". limit: RouterOS rate string e.g. "10,5:packet". tcp_flags: RouterOS flag expression e.g. "syn,!ack". Pass "" to clear an optional field (e.g. src_address="").

tools.name: remove_filter_rule
tools.title: Remove Firewall Filter Rule
tools.description: Removes a firewall filter rule from the MikroTik device. rule_id: use the ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0".

tools.name: move_filter_rule
tools.title: Move Filter Rule
tools.description: Moves a firewall filter rule to a different position in the chain. rule_id: use the ID from list output e.g. "*1" or "0". destination: 0-based target position index.

tools.name: enable_filter_rule
tools.title: Enable Filter Rule
tools.description: Enables a firewall filter rule.

tools.name: disable_filter_rule
tools.title: Disable Filter Rule
tools.description: Disables a firewall filter rule.

tools.description: Creates a basic firewall setup with common security rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_nat_rule
tools.title: Create NAT Rule
tools.description: Creates a NAT rule (srcnat or dstnat) on the MikroTik device. to_addresses: single IP or range e.g. "10.0.0.1" or "10.0.0.1-10.0.0.10". to_ports: single port or range e.g. "8080" or "8080-8090". place_before: rule number or ID (*N) to insert before e.g. "0" or "*3".

to_ports.description: Single port or range e.g. "8080" or "8080-8090"

place_before.description: Rule number or ID (*N) to insert before e.g. "0" or "*3"

tools.name: list_nat_rules
tools.title: List NAT Rules
tools.description: Lists NAT rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_nat_rule
tools.title: Get NAT Rule

tools.title: Update NAT Rule

tools.name: remove_nat_rule

tools.name: add_dot1x_server
tools.title: Add Dot1x Server
tools.description:
Adds an 802.1X authenticator (server) on an interface, enforcing port-based network access control against a RADIUS server (`/interface dot1x server`).

Notes:
    auth_types: 'dot1x' (EAP supplicant), 'mac-auth' (MAC bypass), or
        both.
    guest_vlan_id / reject_vlan_id / server_fail_vlan_id: VLAN to assign
        when there is no supplicant, on auth failure, or when RADIUS is
        unreachable (number, or 'none').

tools.name: update_dot1x_server
tools.title: Update Dot1x Server
tools.description: Updates an 802.1X authenticator (by interface or '.id'). Pass comment="" to clear the comment.

tools.name: remove_dot1x_server
tools.title: Remove Dot1x Server
tools.description: Removes an 802.1X authenticator by interface or '.id' from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: add_dot1x_client
tools.title: Add Dot1x Client
tools.description:
Adds an 802.1X supplicant (client) on an interface so the device can authenticate itself to an upstream authenticator (`/interface dot1x client`).

Notes:
    eap_methods: comma-separated, e.g. 'eap-tls', 'eap-peap',
        'eap-mschapv2', 'eap-ttls'.
    certificate: required for eap-tls; identity/password for the
        password-based methods.

tools.title: Start Sniffer
tools.description: Starts the packet sniffer capturing into memory (and a file if configured). Stop it with stop_sniffer to read the results.

tools.name: stop_sniffer
tools.title: Stop Sniffer
tools.description: Stops the packet sniffer.

tools.name: save_sniffer
tools.title: Save Sniffer Capture
tools.description: Saves the current sniffer buffer to a .pcap file on the device.

tools.name: list_sniffer_packets
tools.title: List Sniffer Packets
tools.description: Lists captured packets (`/tool sniffer packet`).

tools.name: list_sniffer_hosts
tools.title: List Sniffer Hosts
tools.description: Lists hosts seen by the sniffer with byte/packet counts (`/tool sniffer host`).

tools.name: list_sniffer_protocols
tools.title: List Sniffer Protocols
tools.description: Lists the protocol distribution seen by the sniffer (`/tool sniffer protocol`).

tools.name: list_sniffer_connections
tools.title: List Sniffer Connections
tools.description: Lists connections observed by the sniffer (`/tool sniffer connection`).

tools.name: profile_cpu
tools.title: Profile CPU
tools.description: Profiles CPU usage by process/classifier over a bounded sampling window, showing which subsystems consume CPU (`/tool profile`). The run is bounded by `duration`, so it terminates rather than streaming.

duration.description: Sampling duration in seconds (bounds the run)

cpu.description: Limit to a specific CPU core (e.g. '0'), or 'all'

tools.name: get_romon
tools.title: Get RoMON
tools.description: Gets the RoMON settings of the MikroTik device (`/tool romon`).

tools.name: update_romon
tools.title: Update RoMON
tools.description:
Updates the RoMON settings of the MikroTik device.

Notes:
    enabled: turn the Layer-2 RoMON management overlay on/off.
    id: RoMON ID (defaults to a MAC); secrets: shared secret(s) for the
        overlay.

tools.name: add_romon_port
tools.title: Add RoMON Port
tools.description: Adds a RoMON port entry controlling which interfaces participate in the overlay (`/tool romon port`).

interface.description: Interface, or 'all' for the default entry

tools.name: list_romon_ports
tools.title: List RoMON Ports
tools.description: Lists RoMON port entries (`/tool romon port`).

tools.name: remove_romon_port
tools.title: Remove RoMON Port
tools.description: Removes a RoMON port entry by interface or '.id' from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_sms_settings
tools.title: Get SMS Settings
tools.description: Gets the SMS settings of the MikroTik device (`/tool sms`).

tools.name: update_sms_settings
tools.title: Update SMS Settings
tools.description:
Updates the SMS settings of the MikroTik device.

Notes:
    port: the LTE/modem interface used for SMS, e.g. 'lte1'.
    receive_enabled: store incoming SMS in the inbox.
    allowed_number: only act on remote commands from this number.

tools.name: send_sms
tools.title: Send SMS
tools.description: Sends an SMS message via the device's LTE/modem (`/tool sms send`).

tools.name: list_sms_inbox
tools.title: List SMS Inbox
tools.description: Lists received SMS messages (`/tool sms inbox`).

tools.name: speed_test
tools.title: Speed Test
tools.description: Runs a latency and throughput test to another reachable RouterOS device (`/tool speed-test`). The run is bounded by `duration`, so it terminates rather than streaming.

duration.description: Test duration in seconds (bounds the run)

tools.name: add_traffic_generator_port
tools.title: Add Traffic Generator Port
tools.description: Adds a traffic-generator port binding a name to a physical interface (`/tool traffic-generator port`).

tools.name: list_traffic_generator_ports
tools.title: List Traffic Generator Ports
tools.description: Lists traffic-generator ports (`/tool traffic-generator port`).

tools.name: remove_traffic_generator_port
tools.title: Remove Traffic Generator Port
tools.description: Removes a traffic-generator port by name or '.id' from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: add_traffic_generator_stream
tools.title: Add Traffic Generator Stream
tools.description:
Adds a traffic-generator stream describing what to transmit on a port (`/tool traffic-generator stream`).

Notes:
    tx_template: name of an existing packet-template to send.
    tx_rate: transmit rate in bits/second.

tools.name: list_traffic_generator_streams
tools.title: List Traffic Generator Streams
tools.description: Lists traffic-generator streams (`/tool traffic-generator stream`).

tools.name: remove_traffic_generator_stream
tools.title: Remove Traffic Generator Stream
tools.description: Removes a traffic-generator stream by name or '.id' from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: start_traffic_generator
tools.title: Start Traffic Generator
tools.description: Starts the traffic generator, transmitting the configured streams (`/tool traffic-generator start`).

duration.description: Optional run duration in seconds (else runs until stopped)

tools.name: stop_traffic_generator
tools.title: Stop Traffic Generator
tools.description: Stops the traffic generator (`/tool traffic-generator stop`).

tools.name: add_traffic_monitor
tools.title: Add Traffic Monitor
tools.description:
Adds a traffic-monitor entry that runs a script when an interface's traffic crosses a threshold (`/tool traffic-monitor`).

Notes:
    traffic: which direction to watch (received or transmitted).
    trigger: fire when traffic goes 'above' or 'below' the threshold
        (or 'always').
    threshold: rate in bits/second.

on_event.description: Script source/name to run on trigger

tools.name: list_traffic_monitors
tools.title: List Traffic Monitors
tools.description: Lists traffic-monitor entries (`/tool traffic-monitor`).

tools.name: get_traffic_monitor
tools.title: Get Traffic Monitor
tools.description: Gets a specific traffic-monitor entry by name.

tools.name: update_traffic_monitor
tools.title: Update Traffic Monitor
tools.description: Updates a traffic-monitor entry by name. Pass comment="" to clear the comment.

tools.name: remove_traffic_monitor
tools.title: Remove Traffic Monitor
tools.description: Removes a traffic-monitor entry by name.

tools.name: enable_traffic_monitor
tools.title: Enable Traffic Monitor
tools.description: Enables a traffic-monitor entry by name.

tools.name: disable_traffic_monitor
tools.title: Disable Traffic Monitor
tools.description: Disables a traffic-monitor entry by name.

tools.name: wake_on_lan
tools.title: Wake-on-LAN
tools.description: Sends a Wake-on-LAN magic packet to wake a host by MAC address (`/tool wol`).

interface.description: Interface to send the magic packet from

tools.name: create_scheduler
tools.title: Create Scheduler
tools.description: Creates a scheduled task on the MikroTik device that runs an on-event script at an interval or start time.

on_event.description: Script source to run on event (may contain spaces/semicolons)

tools.name: list_schedulers
tools.title: List Schedulers
tools.description: Lists scheduled tasks on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_scheduler
tools.title: Get Scheduler
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific scheduler entry.

tools.name: remove_scheduler
tools.title: Remove Scheduler
tools.description: Removes a scheduled task from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: enable_scheduler
tools.title: Enable Scheduler
tools.description: Enables a scheduled task on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: disable_scheduler
tools.title: Disable Scheduler
tools.description: Disables a scheduled task on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: add_script
tools.title: Add Script
tools.description: Adds a named script to the MikroTik device's script repository.

dont_require_permissions.description: Run without checking the policy permissions of the caller

tools.name: list_scripts
tools.title: List Scripts
tools.description: Lists scripts in the MikroTik device's script repository.

tools.name: remove_script
tools.title: Remove Script
tools.description: Removes a script from the MikroTik device's script repository.

tools.name: run_script
tools.title: Run Script
tools.description: Runs a named script from the MikroTik device's script repository.

tools.name: add_user
tools.title: Add User
tools.description: Adds a user to MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_users
tools.title: List Users
tools.description: Lists users on MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_user
tools.title: Get User
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific user.

tools.name: update_user
tools.title: Update User
tools.description: Updates a user.

tools.name: remove_user
tools.title: Remove User
tools.description: Removes a user.

tools.name: disable_user
tools.title: Disable User
tools.description: Disables a user.

tools.name: enable_user
tools.title: Enable User
tools.description: Enables a user.

tools.name: add_user_group
tools.title: Add User Group
tools.description: Adds a user group.

tools.name: list_user_groups
tools.title: List User Groups
tools.description: Lists user groups on MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_user_group
tools.title: Get User Group
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific user group.

tools.name: update_user_group
tools.title: Update User Group
tools.description: Updates a user group.

tools.name: remove_user_group
tools.title: Remove User Group
tools.description: Removes a user group.

tools.name: get_active_users
tools.title: Active Users
tools.description: Gets currently active/logged-in users.

tools.name: disconnect_user
tools.title: Disconnect User
tools.description: Disconnects an active user session.

tools.name: export_user_config
tools.title: Export User Config
tools.description: Exports user configuration to a file.

tools.name: set_user_ssh_keys
tools.title: Set User SSH Keys
tools.description: Sets SSH keys for a specific user.

tools.name: list_user_ssh_keys
tools.title: List User SSH Keys
tools.description: Lists SSH keys for a specific user.

tools.name: remove_user_ssh_key
tools.title: Remove User SSH Key
tools.description: Removes an SSH key.

tools.name: get_logs
tools.title: Get Logs
tools.description: Gets logs from the MikroTik device with optional topic, time, and message filters.

tools.name: get_logs_by_severity
tools.title: Get Logs by Severity
tools.description: Gets logs filtered by severity level (debug/info/warning/error/critical).

tools.name: get_logs_by_topic
tools.title: Get Logs by Topic
tools.description: Gets logs for a specific topic/facility (system, dhcp, interface, firewall, etc.).

tools.name: search_logs
tools.title: Search Logs
tools.description: Searches log messages for a specific term.

tools.name: get_system_events
tools.title: System Events
tools.description: Gets system-related log events (login, reboot, config-change, etc.).

tools.name: get_security_logs
tools.title: Security Logs
tools.description: Gets security-related log entries (login failures, blocked connections, etc.).

tools.name: clear_logs
tools.title: Clear Logs
tools.description: Clears all logs from the MikroTik device. This action cannot be undone.

tools.name: get_log_statistics
tools.title: Log Statistics
tools.description: Gets log entry counts by topic and severity from the MikroTik device.

tools.name: export_logs
tools.title: Export Logs
tools.description: Exports logs to a file on the MikroTik device with optional topic and time filters.

tools.name: monitor_logs
tools.title: Monitor Logs
tools.description: Monitors MikroTik logs in near-real-time for a limited duration (max 60s).

tools.name: add_logging_rule
tools.title: Add Logging Rule
tools.description: Adds a system logging rule that routes log messages matching the given topics to a logging action.

prefix.description: Text prepended to each matching log message

tools.name: list_logging_rules
tools.title: List Logging Rules
tools.description: Lists system logging rules on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: remove_logging_rule
tools.title: Remove Logging Rule
tools.description: Removes a system logging rule by its internal id.

rule_id.description: Internal .id of the logging rule, e.g. '*1'

tools.name: add_logging_action
tools.title: Add Logging Action
tools.description: Adds a system logging action defining where matching log messages are sent (memory, disk, echo, remote syslog or email).

name.description: Name for the new logging action

tools.name: list_logging_actions
tools.title: List Logging Actions
tools.description: Lists system logging actions on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: remove_logging_action
tools.title: Remove Logging Action
tools.description: Removes a system logging action by name.

name.description: Name of the logging action to remove

tools.name: create_backup
tools.title: Create Backup
tools.description: Creates a system backup on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_backups
tools.title: List Backups
tools.description: Lists backup files on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: create_export
tools.title: Create Config Export
tools.description: Creates a configuration export file (rsc/json/xml) on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: export_section
tools.title: Export Config Section
tools.description: Exports a specific RouterOS configuration section to a file. section: RouterOS path without leading slash e.g. "ip address", "interface vlan", "ip firewall filter", "ip firewall nat", "queue simple".

tools.name: download_file
tools.title: Download File
tools.description: Downloads a backup or export file from the MikroTik device as base64-encoded content.

tools.name: upload_file
tools.title: Upload File
tools.description: Uploads a base64-encoded file to the MikroTik device (for restore operations).

tools.name: restore_backup
tools.title: Restore Backup
tools.description: Restores a system backup on the MikroTik device; triggers a reboot.

tools.name: import_configuration
tools.title: Import Configuration
tools.description: Imports and executes a RouterOS configuration script (.rsc file) on the device.

tools.name: remove_file
tools.title: Remove File
tools.description: Removes a file from the MikroTik device filesystem.

tools.name: backup_info
tools.title: Backup File Info
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a backup file on the MikroTik device.

tools.name: list_disks
tools.title: List Disks
tools.description: Lists storage disks (USB, NVMe, internal flash) attached to the MikroTik device.

tools.name: get_disk
tools.title: Get Disk
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific disk.

tools.name: format_disk
tools.title: Format Disk
tools.description: Formats (ERASES) a disk on the MikroTik device. This destroys all data on the disk. Requires confirm=true.

confirm.description: Must be true to actually erase the disk

tools.name: safe_mode_status
tools.title: Safe Mode Status
tools.description: Returns whether MikroTik Safe Mode is currently active (for the targeted device).

tools.name: enable_safe_mode
tools.title: Enable Safe Mode
tools.description: Activates MikroTik Safe Mode on the targeted device; changes are held in memory and auto-reverted on disconnect until committed.

tools.name: commit_safe_mode
tools.title: Commit Safe Mode
tools.description: Commits all pending Safe Mode changes on the targeted device to persistent storage and exits Safe Mode.

tools.name: rollback_safe_mode
tools.title: Rollback Safe Mode
tools.description: Discards all pending Safe Mode changes on the targeted device by closing the SSH session, triggering automatic rollback.
